creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text:
Is it? Looking at their site, it looks like at least the Mac OS X package and
the Debian package are bundling ruby 2.0, not 2.1. Are you sure 2.1 is required?

Build testing the latest shar now, with some slight changes, btw.

Steve

On Mon, Mar 17, 2014 at 12:05:12PM -0700, Joseph Benden wrote:
> It turns out that Ruby 2.1 is REQUIRED in order for the code to work.
> I've modified the Makefile to IGNORE any ruby but 2.1. I think it's the
> best we can do. Thoughts?
> 
> -Joe
> 
> > Steve Wills <mailto:swills@FreeBSD.org>
> > March 17, 2014 at 10:27 AM
> > On Mon, Mar 17, 2014 at 09:56:58AM -0700, Joseph Benden wrote:
> >> Steve Wills wrote:
> >>> Ok. But using the source from github doesn't mean we can't use the GEM stuff in
> >>> ports, I think. I'll have to look at that more closely. If not, no big deal.
> >> I didn't see a way to use parts of the makefiles, so I mirrored the
> >> parts I needed.
> >
> > Ok, sounds fine then.
> >
> >>>>> PS: It was pointed out on the vagrant mailing list that versions 1.4 or
> >>>>> higher require Ruby 2.0 or newer. So yes, we'd have to depend on Ruby 2.1.
> >>> This is the bigger issue. Technically, right now, we only have gem19. If you
> >>> specify ruby 2.0 or 2.1, that's fine, but you don't get a GEMBIN to go with it.
> >>> You only get that for the default ruby.
> >> But if the port is installed, gem21 is automatically found and used. I
> >> assume that the build process doesn't follow the same procedures as
> >> making the package inside of ports?
> >
> > Sure, but here's what happens in poudriere:
> >
> > http://meatwad.mouf.net/poudriere/logs/bulk/11i386-default/2014-03-17_17h13m06s/logs/errors/vagrant-1.5.1.log
> >
> > ===>  Building for vagrant-1.5.1
> > env: gem21: No such file or directory
> >
> > because my default ruby is 2.0 and only gem20 exists. If it were the ports
> > default ruby 1.9, it would be gem19. And of course just calling "gem" won't
> > help because that will install the gems for the wrong version of ruby...
> >
> >>> This is what failed for me in my initial test of this using poudriere. This is
> >>> deficiency in ports, but it's one we'll have to deal with, either by fixing
> >>> ports or some change to the vagrant port.
> >> Is there something I can do to help?
> >
> > See below.
> >
> >>> I suppose one ugly way would be to IGNORE if the default ruby version wasn't
> >>> 2.0 or 2.1. This way the port would be useful for those who build their own
> >>> packages with default ruby set to 2.0 or 2.1, but others wouldn't get an error
> >>> and we wouldn't get errors from official package building. Not ideal, but at
> >>> least gets it in.
> >> Could IGNORE Ruby 1.9 and offer an option for whether the user wants to
> >> build against 2.0 or 2.1. I believe it is best to leave it with 2.1, but
> >> I'm probably being biased.
> >
> > I think what you're going to have to do is check that the *default* ruby version
> > is 2.1 and if not, just IGNORE, at least for now. RUBY_VER doesn't need to be
> > set in that case. Long term, hacking things up to install different gems for
> > various rubies will be needed.
> >
> > BTW, there's no need to support NO_STAGE.
> >
> > Steve
> >
> > Joseph Benden <mailto:joe@thrallingpenguin.com>
> > March 17, 2014 at 9:56 AM

> >> This is the bigger issue. Technically, right now, we only have gem19. If you
> >> specify ruby 2.0 or 2.1, that's fine, but you don't get a GEMBIN to go with it.
> >> You only get that for the default ruby.
> > But if the port is installed, gem21 is automatically found and used. I
> > assume that the build process doesn't follow the same procedures as
> > making the package inside of ports?
> >> This is what failed for me in my initial test of this using poudriere. This is
> >> deficiency in ports, but it's one we'll have to deal with, either by fixing
> >> ports or some change to the vagrant port.
> >
> > Is there something I can do to help?
> >
> >> I suppose one ugly way would be to IGNORE if the default ruby version wasn't
> >> 2.0 or 2.1. This way the port would be useful for those who build their own
> >> packages with default ruby set to 2.0 or 2.1, but others wouldn't get an error
> >> and we wouldn't get errors from official package building. Not ideal, but at
> >> least gets it in.
> >
> > Could IGNORE Ruby 1.9 and offer an option for whether the user wants
> > to build against 2.0 or 2.1. I believe it is best to leave it with
> > 2.1, but I'm probably being biased.
> >
> > -Joe

> >> It looks like we will have to use my more complicated build scripts. The
> >> gem available from RG is a "filler" saying that the gem is no longer
> >> updated with RG and that you should install using packages available at
> >> http://vagrantup.com - which of course there is no FreeBSD package.
> >>
> >> I'm recommending we use my more complex build files. Thoughts?
> >
> > Ok. But using the source from github doesn't mean we can't use the GEM stuff in
> > ports, I think. I'll have to look at that more closely. If not, no big deal.
> >
> >> PS: It was pointed out on the vagrant mailing list that versions 1.4 or
> >> higher require Ruby 2.0 or newer. So yes, we'd have to depend on Ruby 2.1.
> >
> > This is the bigger issue. Technically, right now, we only have gem19. If you
> > specify ruby 2.0 or 2.1, that's fine, but you don't get a GEMBIN to go with it.
> > You only get that for the default ruby.
> >
> > This is what failed for me in my initial test of this using poudriere. This is
> > deficiency in ports, but it's one we'll have to deal with, either by fixing
> > ports or some change to the vagrant port.
> >
> > I suppose one ugly way would be to IGNORE if the default ruby version wasn't
> > 2.0 or 2.1. This way the port would be useful for those who build their own
> > packages with default ruby set to 2.0 or 2.1, but others wouldn't get an error
> > and we wouldn't get errors from official package building. Not ideal, but at
> > least gets it in.
> >
> > Steve

> > I'm recommending we use my more complex build files. Thoughts?

> > higher require Ruby 2.0 or newer. So yes, we'd have to depend on Ruby 2.1.

> > Awesome, this looks really great! I'm going to build test this and commit ASAP.
> >
> > Thanks!
> > Steve

> > On Mon, Mar 17, 2014 at 08:21:46AM -0700, Joseph Benden wrote:
> >> Steve Wills wrote:
> >>> Since RUBY_VER=2.1, does this mean the port only works with Ruby 2.1?
> >> The latest version, 1.5.1, only works with newer rubies.
> >>
> >>> Also, why not use USE_RUBYGEMS=yes and RUBYGEM_AUTOPLIST=yes? 
> >> Well, up until a day ago, RubyGems wasn't updated to any recent release.
> >> I had to build the gem from source. Now an older 1.5.0 version is
> >> available on RubyGems. Please find the attached patch that uses
> >> USE_RUBYGEMS=yes and RUBYGEM_AUTOPLIST=yes. This is a point release
> >> behind what's available, though. So we could use the original method I
> >> have in the Makefile to build it from source.
> >>
> >>> Finally, are you
> >>> sure emulators is the right category for this? Perhaps sysutils would be
> >>> better?
> >> I switched it to sysutils. I had it under emulators because it's a tool
> >> that works with emulators...
> >>
> >>
> >
